definitions.definition: Saturated hydrocarbons that contain only single bonds between carbon atoms. They are also known as paraffins. They have the general formula CnH2n+2.
definitions.example: Methane (CH4) is an example of an alkane.

definitions.definition: Unsaturated hydrocarbons that contain at least one double bond between carbon atoms. They have the general formula CnH2n.
definitions.example: Ethene (C2H4) is an example of an alkene.

definitions.definition: Unsaturated hydrocarbons that contain at least one triple bond between carbon atoms. They have the general formula CnH2n-2.
definitions.example: Ethyne (C2H2) is an example of an alkyne.

definitions.definition: Hydrocarbons that contain only single bonds between carbon atoms.
definitions.example: Alkanes are examples of saturated hydrocarbons.

definitions.definition: Hydrocarbons that contain one or more multiple bonds between carbon atoms.
definitions.example: Alkenes and alkynes are examples of unsaturated hydrocarbons.

definitions.definition: The occurrence of two or more compounds with the same molecular formula but different structural formulas.
A group of atoms within a molecule that determines its chemical properties.
definitions.example: The -CH3 group is a functional group in alkane molecules.

definitions.definition: The addition of hydrogen to a molecule, typically in the presence of a catalyst.
definitions.example: The hydrogenation of alkenes results in the formation of alkanes.

definitions.definition: A process that involves the breaking of large hydrocarbon molecules into smaller molecules.
definitions.example: The cracking of petroleum produces lighter hydrocarbons.

definitions.definition: A process that involves the conversion of carbon monoxide and hydrogen into hydrocarbons.
definitions.example: The Fischer-Tropsch process is used to produce liquid fuels.

definitions.definition: A naturally occurring mixture of hydrocarbons that can be refined into various petroleum products.
definitions.example: Petroleum is a major source of energy.

definitions.definition: A naturally occurring mixture of hydrocarbons and other gases that can be used as a fuel.
definitions.example: Natural gas is a cleaner-burning fuel than coal or oil.

definitions.definition: A dark liquid that is produced from the distillation of coal and contains various organic compounds.
definitions.example: Coal tar is used to produce dyes and other chemicals.

definitions.definition: A mixture of hydrocarbons that is produced from the distillation of petroleum.
definitions.example: Naphtha is used as a solvent and in the production of plastics.

definitions.definition: A process that involves the separation of a mixture of liquids based on their boiling points.
definitions.example: Fractional distillation is used to separate the components of petroleum.

definitions.definition: A process that involves the conversion of a hydrocarbon into a more useful product, typically in the presence of a catalyst.
definitions.example: The reforming of naphtha produces high-octane gasoline.

definitions.definition: A process that involves the cracking of hydrocarbons in the presence of a catalyst.
definitions.example: Catalytic cracking is used to produce lighter hydrocarbons.
